2606.22126.title: From Recognition to Understanding: Unlocking Cognitive Time Series Reasoning with LLMs
2606.22126.paper_summary: Time series analysis has recently been coupled with Large Language Models (LLMs) to leverage their reasoning and world knowledge capabilities, yet gains remain limited. We attribute this to a fundamental mismatch between existing task formulations and LLM strengths: most settings reduce time series understanding to curve-fitting systems, focusing on low-level prediction while ignoring the semantic, contextual, and reasoning-intensive nature of real-world temporal decision-making.To address these limitations, we introduce TSCognition, a multimodal benchmark for multi-dimensional time series reasoning. It collects real-world time series and textual information from 15 public sources and constructs approximately 41K QA samples around five cognitive reasoning tasks: Decoding, Grounding, Inferring, Extrapolating, and Acting. Building on this, we further propose TSAlign, a unified framework that encodes time series into compact patch-level representations and aligns them with semantic directions in the LLM embedding space via gated residual injection and multivariate fusion.Experiments show that TSAlign outperforms existing LLM, VLM, and time series QA baselines on TSCognition and the publicly available TimerBed benchmark while substantially reducing computational cost.Code is available at: [https://github.com/EIT-NLP/CognitiveTSR](https://github.com/EIT-NLP/CognitiveTSR)
2606.22126.paper_summary_zh: 時間序列分析最近與大型語言模型（LLMs）結合，以利用其推理和世界知識能力，但收益仍然有限。我們將此歸因於現有任務表述與LLM優勢之間的根本不匹配：大多數設置將時間序列理解簡化為曲線擬合系統，專注於低層次的預測，同時忽略了現實世界時間決策的語義、上下文和推理密集性質。為了解決這些限制，我們介紹了TSCognition，一個多模態基準，用於多維時間序列推理。它從15個公共來源收集現實世界的時間序列和文本信息，並圍繞五個認知推理任務構建約41K的問答樣本：解碼、基礎、推斷、外推和行動。在此基礎上，我們進一步提出了TSAlign，一個統一框架，將時間序列編碼為緊湊的補丁級表示，並通過門控殘差注入和多變量融合將其與LLM嵌入空間中的語義方向對齊。實驗表明，TSAlign在TSCognition和公開可用的TimerBed基準上超越了現有的LLM、VLM和時間序列問答基準，同時顯著降低了計算成本。代碼可在以下網址獲得：[https://github.com/EIT-NLP/CognitiveTSR](https://github.com/EIT-NLP/CognitiveTSR)
2606.22126.author: Xin Qiu et.al.
2606.22126.authors: Xin Qiu, Junlong Tong, Yao Zhang, Yunpu Ma, Wei Zhang, Xiaoyu Shen
